title: Les pronoms COD/COI — Object pronouns
objective: Replace nouns with object pronouns to avoid repetition (A2: can use pronouns in connected speech).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: the object pronoun comes BEFORE the verb
bridge.body: Direct objects use **le / la / les** (him/her/it/them); indirect objects (with **à**) use **lui** (to him/her) / **leur** (to them). Unlike English (“I see **him**” — after), French puts them **before** the verb: *Je **le** vois.* *Je **lui** parle.* Hindi word order actually helps — the object usually comes before the verb there too (मैं उसे देखता हूँ).

quiz.prompt: What is the main bridge idea for "Les pronoms COD/COI — Object pronouns"?
options: Focus on: the object pronoun comes BEFORE the verb

example.french: Je le vois souvent au bureau.
example.englishGloss: I see him often at the office.

glosses.en: I see him often at the office.

example.french: Je lui donne un cadeau pour Diwali.
example.englishGloss: I give her a gift for Diwali.

glosses.en: I give her a gift for Diwali.
glosses.te: దీపావళి కోసం నేను ఆమెకు బహుమతి ఇస్తాను.

example.englishGloss: I talk to them in French.

glosses.en: I talk to them in French.

example.french: Le cadeau est pour ma sœur.
example.englishGloss: The gift is for my sister.

glosses.en: The gift is for my sister.

example.french: J'écris une lettre à mon ami.
example.englishGloss: I write a letter to my friend.

glosses.en: I write a letter to my friend.

exercises.prompt: “I see him often.” → « Je ___ vois souvent. »
exercises.explanation: Direct object “him” = le, placed before the verb.
